evidences.title: Extract Historical Data from Binance Using Python | Medium
evidences.content: from binance.client import Clientimport pandas as pdimport datetime# Binance API credentialsapi_key = 'your_api_key'api_secret = 'your_api_secret'client = Client(api_key, api_secret)# Define the symbol for BTC/USDT pairsymbol = 'BTCUSDT'# Define custom start and end timestart_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 3, 15, 0, 0, 0)end_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 15, 0, 0, 0)klines = client.get_historical_klines(symbol=symbol, interval=Client.KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, start_str=str(start_time), end_str=str(end_time))# Convert the data into a pandas dataframe for easier manipulationdf_M = pd.DataFrame(klines, columns=['Open Time', 'Open', 'High', 'Low', 'Close', 'Volume', 'Close Time', 'Quote Asset Volume', 'Number of Trades', 'Taker Buy Base Asset Volume', 'Taker Buy Quote Asset Volume', [...] ``` symbol = 'BTCUSDT' 'BTCUSDT' ``` We can set a custom start and end time for our function by parsing the date to date time format. ``` # format (year, month, day, hour, minute, second)start_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 3, 15, 0, 0, 0)end_time = datetime.datetime(2024, 6, 15, 0, 0, 0) # format (year, month, day, hour, minute, second) 2024 3 15 0 0 0 2024 6 15 0 0 0 ``` `datetime.datetime`: Cr...

evidences.content: `symbol` and `limit` are straightforward, `startTime` and `endTime` we now have covered by the above `date_to_milliseconds` function. We note from this that we can get a maximum of 500 results each request, so we will need to build a loop if we are fetching over a long time period. `interval` we have a list of so these are passed easily. The get\_klines endpoint returns an array of klines in this order ``` [1499040000000, # Open time "0.01634790", # Open "0.80000000", # High "0.01575800", # Low "0.01577100", # Close "148976.11427815", # Volume 1499644799999, # Close time "2434.19055334", # Quote asset volume 308, # Number of trades "1756.87402397", # Taker buy base asset volume "28.46694368", # Taker buy quote asset volume "17928899.62484339" # Ignore ] ``` [...] ``` frombinance.client import Client # fetch 1 minute klines for the last day up until now klines = get_historical_klines("BNBBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_1MINUTE, "1 day ago UTC") # fetch 30 minute klines for the last month of 2017 klines = get_historical_klines("ETHBTC", Client. KLINE_INTERVAL_30MINUTE,"1 Dec, 2017","1 Jan, 2018") # fetch weekly klines since it listed klines = get_historical_klines("NEOBTC", KLINE_INT...

evidences.title: Get historical klines from binance api - GitHub
evidences.content: #### CLI Options | Option | Alias | Description | --- | `--pair` | `-p` | Trading pair (e.g., BTCUSDT, ETHUSDT) | | `--interval` | `-i` | Kline interval (1m, 3m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 4h, 6h, 8h, 12h, 1d, 3d, 1w) | | `--start` | `-s` | Start date (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601) | | `--end` | `-e` | End date (YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601) | | `--output` | `-o` | Output directory path (filename is auto-generated) | | `--format` | `-f` | Output format (json or csv, default: json) | | `--help` | `-h` | Display help | | `--version` | `-V` | Display version | #### Hybrid mode You can also provide some options and be prompted for the rest: ``` # You will be prompted for start date, end date, and output path # ``` ### Library [...] ## Installation ### Via Homebrew (macOS/Linux) ``` brew install maxgfr/tap/binance-historical ``` ### Via npm ``` npm install -g binance-historical ``` ### Download binary Download the latest binary for your platform from the releases page. ## Usage ### CLI #### Interactive mode ``` binance-historical download ``` #### Non-interactive mode Pass all options as arguments: ``` binance-historical download \ --pair BTCUSDT \ --interval 4h \ --start 2024-01-01 \ --end 2024-12-3...

evidences.title: ET to UTC Converter - Time.now
evidences.content: Time Difference: UTC is 5 hours ahead of EST (winter) and 4 hours ahead of EDT (summer). The difference changes twice yearly with daylight saving transitions. #### Daylight Saving Time Impact Winter Period (EST): From first Sunday in November to second Sunday in March, ET is UTC-5, making UTC 5 hours ahead Summer Period (EDT): From second Sunday in March to first Sunday in November, ET is UTC-4, making UTC 4 hours ahead UTC Stability: UTC never changes and remains constant year-round, making it ideal for international coordination and technical applications #### Common Conversion Examples Business Hours (Winter) EST 9:00 AM → UTC 2:00 PM EST 5:00 PM → UTC 10:00 PM 5-hour difference during winter Business Hours (Summer) EDT 9:00 AM → UTC 1:00 PM EDT 5:00 PM → UTC 9:00 PM [...] Whether you're in New York trying to schedule a call with someone managing a server in Iceland, or you're working remotely across borders, time zone confusion leads to missed meetings and delayed responses. This converter bridges that Gap cleanly. It accounts for daylight saving changes, includes a live clock for both ET and UTC, and updates automatically when you change inputs. That means no more second-gu...

evidences.content: Kline/Candlestick data `GET /api/v1/klines Kline/candlestick bars for a symbol. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. Weight: 1 Parameters: Name Type Mandatory Description symbol STRING YES interval ENUM YES startTime LONG NO endTime LONG NO limit INT NO Default 500; max 1000. If startTime and endTime are not sent, the most recent klines are returned. Response: [ [ 1499040000000, // Open time "0.01634790", // Open "0.80000000", // High "0.01575800", // Low "0.01577100", // Close "148976.11427815", // Volume 1499644799999, // Close time "2434.19055334", // Quote asset volume 308, // Number of trades "1756.87402397", // Taker buy base asset volume "28.46694368", // Taker buy quote asset volume "17928899.62484339" // Ignore. ] ]` JBaczuk's user avatar ## Your Answer

evidences.title: EST to UTC
evidences.content: Based International Air Transport Association (IATA) timezone codes. Time zones use hours and minutes offset from Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), most a whole number of hours, a few are offset by 30 or 45 minutes. Eastern Standard Time (EST) is UTC-5:00, and Eastern Daylight Time (EDT) is UTC-4:00, this time zone is called Eastern Time Zone (ET) in the United States, parts of eastern Canada, Mexico, Panama and the Caribbean Islands. Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) is the primary time standard now, time zones around the world are expressed using offsets from UTC, UTC offset is the difference in hours and minutes from UTC, a time zone can be determined by adding or subtracting the number of UTC offset. [...] Converter supports daylight saving time (DST) or summer time, if the target location is currently using DST time zone, converter will automatically calculate and display local summer time. evidences.title: Time Zone Converter & Calculator - GIGACalculator.com
evidences.content: For example, to convert Eastern Time to GMT, enter the date and time, select "UTC-5 Eastern Time" in the "From" drop-down, then select "UTC+0 (GMT)" in the "To" drop-down, and the resulting date and time in GMT will be displayed in the results fields. ## US time zone map The USA spans a lot of the Earth's surface longitudinally so it uses a total of nine time zones across its territory. The most used are Eastern Time (ET), Central Time (CT), Mountain Time (MT), and Pacific Time (PT). The standard UTC offsets are denoted EST, CST, MST, and PST, whereas the time zone offsets during DST (Daylight Saving Time) are denoted EDT, CDT, MDT, and PDT. [...] ## Time difference and Daylight Savings Time (DST) While the time difference between any two standard time zones is always the same, certain countries or states may switch time zones while observing daylight saving (DST), making the calculations to convert time zones internationally more difficult. Most countries that observe daylight saving are in North America (most of the US and Canada, Cuba), Europe (the vast majority of European countries follow DST), New Zealand, and some Australian states. The only Asian country to have DST is I...

evidences.title: EST to UTC Converter - Current Time UTC
evidences.content: ### What is the difference between EST and EDT? EDT (Eastern Daylight Time) is used during Daylight Saving time and is UTC-4 hours. EST (Eastern Standard Time) is used outside of Daylight Saving Time and is UTC-5 hours. ### What are some major industries in the EST time zone? [...] ### What are the national holidays in the EST time zone? National holidays: New Year's Day: Celebrated on January 1st, marking the start of the new year. Independence Day: Observed on July 4th, with parades, fireworks, and celebrations throughout the region. Labor Day: Celebrated on the first Monday in September, honoring American workers and their contributions. Thanksgiving Day: Observed on the fourth Thursday in November, a major holiday for family gatherings and feasting. Christmas Day: Celebrated on December 25th, marked by family gatherings, feasts, and festive celebrations. ### What is some local trivia or geography about the EST region? [...] ### What are some major industries in the EST time zone? Major industries: Finance: New York City is a global financial hub, home to the New York Stock Exchange and Wall Street.
